Name: CurvatureAwareRoutingOptimization
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing Optimization for Efficient Machine Learning Training
Short Hypothesis: Can a curvature-aware precision routing framework, which dynamically assigns mixed-precision levels based on gradient curvature and Hessian conditioning, significantly reduce training time and memory usage while maintaining or improving model accuracy?
Related Work: Existing work in mixed-precision optimization, such as Tri-Accel (Sheibanian et al., 2025), focuses on adaptive precision assignment and batch size scaling. However, these methods do not explicitly consider the curvature and Hessian conditioning of the optimization landscape. Our proposal introduces a curvature-aware mechanism that dynamically adjusts precision levels and routing paths based on the geometric properties of the loss function, providing a more fine-grained and theoretically grounded approach.
Abstract: We propose a Curvature-Aware Precision Routing Optimization (CAPRO) framework for efficient machine learning training. CAPRO dynamically assigns mixed-precision levels to layers based on gradient curvature and Hessian conditioning, while also optimizing the routing paths for gradient updates. This approach aims to reduce training time and memory usage while maintaining or improving model accuracy. We validate CAPRO on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets using ResNet-18 and EfficientNet-B0 architectures, demonstrating significant improvements in efficiency and accuracy compared to static mixed-precision training and existing adaptive methods.

Experiments.Description: Evaluate the impact of curvature-aware precision assignment on training time and memory usage.
Experiments.Method: Train ResNet-18 and EfficientNet-B0 on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 using CAPRO and compare against FP32, static mixed-precision, and Tri-Accel. Measure training time, memory usage, and model accuracy.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, model accuracy.

Experiments.Description: Analyze the effect of Hessian conditioning on precision assignment and routing paths.
Experiments.Method: Perform spectral analysis of the Hessian matrix at different training stages and correlate the results with precision assignment and routing decisions. Evaluate the impact on convergence and stability.
Experiments.Metrics: Spectral properties of the Hessian, precision assignment, routing paths, convergence rate, and stability.

Experiments.Description: Conduct ablation studies to isolate the contributions of curvature-aware precision assignment and routing optimization.
Experiments.Method: Train models with and without curvature-aware precision assignment and routing optimization, and compare the results.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, model accuracy, and convergence rate.

Experiments.Description: Evaluate the generalizability of CAPRO across different architectures and datasets.
Experiments.Method: Test CAPRO on additional architectures (e.g., VGG-16, MobileNetV2) and datasets (e.g., ImageNet, MNIST) to assess its robustness and scalability.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, model accuracy, and generalization performance.

Risk Factors and Limitations: The dynamic precision assignment and routing optimization may introduce additional computational overhead, which could offset the gains in training efficiency.
The spectral analysis of the Hessian matrix can be computationally expensive, especially for large models and datasets.
The proposed framework may require careful tuning of hyperparameters to achieve optimal performance, which could be challenging in practice.
The effectiveness of CAPRO may vary across different architectures and datasets, and additional experiments may be needed to validate its generalizability.
